key_points: Erbium lasers (Er:YAG, Er,Cr:YSGG) demonstrate effective and selective caries ablation, smear layer removal, and improved adhesion to dental substrates.
Laser-activated irrigation protocols (PIPS™ and SWEEPS™) enhance irrigant penetration and biofilm disruption in endodontic disinfection.
In prosthodontics and orthodontics, erbium lasers enable minimally invasive debonding of crowns, veneers, and brackets while preserving underlying structures.
In periodontology and peri-implantitis, lasers improve debridement, support tissue regeneration, and enhance wound healing.
Photobiomodulation (PBM/LLLT) contributes to pain reduction, inflammation control, and tissue repair across multiple dental specialties.
Clinical outcomes vary significantly with wavelength, energy settings, and clinical protocols, underscoring the need for standardization.
The review is aligned with the FDI Policy Statement on dental lasers, providing an international policy framework for clinical practice.
Further high-quality randomized controlled trials are necessary to define standardized protocols for laser use in dentistry.
Lasers represent valuable adjuncts to conventional dental treatment by enabling minimally invasive procedures and promoting biological healing.

outcomes.measured: Caries ablation efficacy with erbium lasers
outcomes.result: Effective and selective caries removal with smear layer elimination and improved adhesion
outcomes.clinical_significance: Supports minimally invasive caries management as alternative or adjunct to conventional drilling

outcomes.result: Enhanced irrigant penetration and biofilm disruption compared to conventional irrigation
outcomes.clinical_significance: Clinically relevant improvement in root canal disinfection

outcomes.measured: Debonding of prosthetic and orthodontic elements with erbium lasers
outcomes.result: Minimally invasive debonding of crowns, veneers, and brackets with preservation of underlying structures
outcomes.clinical_significance: Reduces iatrogenic damage during debonding procedures

outcomes.measured: Periodontal and peri-implant outcomes with laser therapy
outcomes.result: Improved debridement, tissue regeneration support, and enhanced wound healing
outcomes.clinical_significance: Adjunctive benefit in periodontal and peri-implant treatment

outcomes.measured: PBM effects on pain, inflammation, and tissue repair
outcomes.result: Positive contributions to pain reduction, inflammation control, and tissue repair
outcomes.clinical_significance: Supports use of PBM as adjunct across multiple dental specialties

notes: Narrative review aligned with FDI Policy Statement. No original clinical data; synthesizes existing evidence across multiple dental specialties. No specific laser parameters reported — protocols are described conceptually without numerical values.
source_citation: Grzech-Leśniak, K. (2026). Lasers in Dentistry - Overview Based on FDI Policy Statement. International Dental Journal. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.identj.2025.109369
clinical_indication: Multiple dental applications: caries removal, endodontic disinfection, periodontal therapy, peri-implant therapy, prosthetic and orthodontic debonding, photobiomodulation
target_tissue: dentin, enamel, pulp, gingiva, bone, periodontal tissue, peri-implant tissue
uncertainties: No specific laser parameters (wavelength, power, fluence, pulse duration) are reported in the abstract; the full text may contain more detail but is not available here.
The review is narrative, so no pooled effect sizes or meta-analytic data are available.
Specific brands or models of lasers are not mentioned in the abstract.
The FDI Policy Statement referenced is not cited explicitly in the abstract, limiting direct cross-referencing.

protocols.name: Láser en Odontología — Visión General Multiespecialidad (Revisión Teórica FDI)
protocols.purpose: Proporcionar una síntesis basada en evidencia de las aplicaciones del láser en odontología (cariología, endodoncia, prostodoncia, ortodoncia, periodoncia y terapia peri-implantaria), alineada con la Declaración de Política de la FDI, para orientar la práctica clínica global.
critical_notes: ⚠️ Este protocolo es de carácter teórico/revisión. No existen parámetros clínicos estandarizados reportados en este documento. Basado en: Grzech-Leśniak (2026).
Los resultados clínicos varían significativamente según la longitud de onda, los ajustes de energía y los protocolos clínicos utilizados; se requiere estandarización.
El documento presenta múltiples aplicaciones favorablemente; existen alternativas convencionales con evidencia establecida que pueden ser apropiadas según el caso clínico.
Se requieren ensayos clínicos aleatorizados de alta calidad para definir protocolos estandarizados en cada especialidad.
PIPS™ y SWEEPS™ son marcas registradas de Fotona. Otros fabricantes ofrecen técnicas fotoacústicas equivalentes con nomenclatura propia.
protocols.pbm_complement: La fotobiomodulación (PBM/LLLT) se menciona como complemento en múltiples especialidades para reducción del dolor, control de la inflamación y reparación tisular. No se especifican parámetros concretos en este documento.
indications: Remoción de caries (ablación selectiva con láseres de erbio)
Desinfección endodóntica mediante irrigación activada por láser (PIPS™, SWEEPS™)
Desbonding de coronas, carillas y brackets con láseres de erbio
Fotobiomodulación para reducción del dolor, inflamación y cicatrización tisular
protocols.evidence_level: Bajo — revisión narrativa teórica. No hay parámetros clínicos estandarizados ni datos originales. Basado en síntesis de estudios existentes alineada con la Declaración de Política de la FDI (Grzech-Leśniak, 2026).
